hero.tagline:
Two orchestras. Three nights. One stadium.
The Asian tango lineup that doesn't exist anywhere else.

orchestras.title: One stage, two orchestras.
orchestras.lede: Two living tango groups, flown directly from Buenos Aires. A lineup that doesn't exist anywhere else in Asia.

items.name: Tango Bardo
items.description: A leading Buenos Aires tango orchestra, blending traditional roots with modern expression.

items.name: Misteriosa Buenos Aires
items.description: A Buenos Aires-based ensemble known for powerful, atmospheric stage performances.

schedule.title: Three days, one breath.
schedule.lede: An opening orchestra concert, six milongas across three days, and a city tour to close.

items.title: Opening Orchestra Concert
items.subtitle: 오프닝 오케스트라 콘서트
items.mood: The festival begins with a live stage.

items.title: M1 · Welcome Milonga
items.subtitle: 환영 밀롱가
items.mood: From sunlight to dusk — the first embrace.

items.title: M2 · First Night
items.subtitle: 첫째 밤 밀롱가
items.mood: The longest, deepest dive of the three days.

items.title: M4 · Grand Milonga
items.subtitle: 그란 밀롱가
items.mood: The peak of the festival. The most luminous night.

items.title: Chuncheon City Tour
items.subtitle: 춘천 시티투어
items.mood: A walk through the lakeside city's autumn together.

items.title: M6 · After Party & Milonga
items.subtitle: 애프터 파티 & 밀롱가
items.mood: One last tanda to close three days.

djs.title: Six DJs.
djs.lede: The flow of the milonga begins at the DJ's fingertips. Six curators invited from Korea, China, and Hong Kong.

dancers.title: Dance Performances
dancers.lede: Four teams representing the Korean tango scene.

venue.title: Bomnae Complex
venue.tagline:
A large space at the city's heart.
Professional sound. Overwhelming scale.

features.text: About 60 minutes from Seoul by ITX
Large dance floor in the city center

about.title: Chuncheon International Tango Festival
about.body: KCCTF is a three-day festival combining live orchestra concerts with social dance (milongas). Two orchestras flown directly from Buenos Aires, six DJs from Korea, China, and Hong Kong — three nights of tango together. A meeting place for the global tango community in Chuncheon's autumn.

items.label: Supported by
items.value: Chuncheon City · Embassy of Argentina in Korea

items.a: Click the Register button to be taken to the application page. Reservation is confirmed upon payment within 7 days of submission.
Please see the Refund Policy page. Partial refund available up to 30 days before the festival; refunds become difficult thereafter.
Shinhan Bank, account 100-038-582544 (holder: Chuncheon International Tango Festival Foundation).

items.q: Can I pay with a foreign credit card?
items.a: Currently we operate primarily with bank transfers. For foreign card payments, please contact info@kcctf.org for separate guidance.

items.q: How do I get to Chuncheon?
items.a: About 60 minutes from Seoul by ITX-Cheongchun or KTX, or about 80 minutes by intercity bus. From Incheon Airport, around 2-3 hours via airport bus or ITX transfer.
The most convenient option is the official hotel (Chuncheon Bella Stay) booked through our Full Pack + 2/3 nights packages. You may also book other accommodations independently.
Workshop availability will be announced soon.
On-site registration is possible but may be limited if capacity is reached. Advance reservation is strongly recommended.
